hq: 989 Market Street, FL #2, San Francisco, CA, 94103

system_description.overview: Perspectives Health provides an AI-powered administrative automation platform that assists behavioral health providers in reducing the burden of clinical documentation and administrative tasks. The system operates as a HIPAA-compliant browser extension that overlays onto existing web-based electronic health record (EHR) systems, enabling automation of documentation workflows without requiring APIs, system migrations, or changes to a clinic's existing infrastructure.

products.AI Clinical Assistant: Automates behavioral health workflows; completes session notes directly in the EMR based on recordings of the session started by the clinician. Supports ASAM assessments, biopsychosocial evaluations, intake forms, progress notes, and group therapy notes.
products.Clinical Director Dashboard: Allows for oversight and management functionality.
products.HIPAA-Compliant Browser Extension: Integrates with any web-based EMR; no APIs or system migrations required; instant deployment within client environment.
products.Implementation and Ongoing Support: Comprehensive onboarding, training, and configuration for clinical teams with dedicated customer success support.

regions: Not explicitly named; DigitalOcean high availability data centers
infrastructure.physical_presence: None - no physical offices; all data processed on cloud services
additional_cloud: Render (listed in metadata as infra_provider but not mentioned in report body)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management. Firewall access restricted to authorized personnel.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via DigitalOcean, used to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through a bastion host with a virtual private network (VPN).
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from an established third-party certificate authority for all web-based systems.
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server.
network_architecture.topology: Chrome Extension / Web Dashboards -> Firewall -> Nginx (443) -> FastAPI (5001) -> PostgreSQL (Docker). Production and UAT droplets each follow this pattern. Both connect to DigitalOcean Spaces S3 Storage. Separate production and UAT environments within DigitalOcean.

application_architecture.environments: Two: Production and UAT (Stage), separated within DigitalOcean

authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform. MFA enforced for all remote access. MFA required for source code tool access. Two-factor authentication required for email access.
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination of employment
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through DigitalOcean password policy; includes periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts. Guest and anonymous logins not allowed.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: Cloud provider (DigitalOcean) key management service; access to encryption keys restricted to authorized personnel

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (name not specified; likely GitHub based on MFA + branch protection references)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move changes to production requires additional approval. Developers cannot make changes to application code in production without additional approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for infrastructure as code; baseline configurations retained within the infrastructure as a code
ci_cd_devops.container_orchestration: Docker (no orchestration platform like K8s/ECS mentioned)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned for patch compliance daily.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not explicitly named; monitoring tool generates alerts when predefined thresholds are met
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not explicitly named; logging enabled to monitor administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure provider (data center services, compute, storage, networking)
Listed as infrastructure provider in metadata (possibly for auxiliary services)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers and Mac-based workstations/laptops. Auto-scan on continuous basis. Virus definitions updated weekly for production servers, daily from vendor for endpoints.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; frequency adjusted as required. Performed by internal IT Infrastructure team.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annually by independent third-party. Results reviewed by IT management. High/critical findings remediated in timely manner.

untestable: CC2.2.3 - Significant changes to people, roles and responsibilities could not be tested because there were no significant changes made during the engagement period
CC3.2.1 (Cybersecurity insurance) - Could not be tested because there were no cyber incidents reported during the engagement period
CC6.5.2 (Customer data deletion upon termination) - Could not be tested because there were no customer terminations during the engagement period
CC7.3.1 (Incident response) - Could not be tested because there were no security incidents reported during the engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access controls excluded; DigitalOcean responsible for physical security of data center
A1.2 - Environmental protections excluded; no physical office, managed by building management / DigitalOcean
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC6.1.1 - MFA enforced for administrative access to production
CC8.1.2 - Branch protection rules require merge request approval before production deployment
CC8.1.3 - Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed; MFA required for source code tool access
CC7.4.1 - BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually with annual testing of loss scenarios
CC7.4.2 - Backups taken daily, multi-AZ configured, alerts for backup failure
CC4.1.1 - Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC6.1.7 - Network segmentation based on sensitivity and criticality
CC9.2.1 - Vendor management policy with annual SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments for critical vendors without SOC reports

infrastructure.rationale: Single cloud provider (DigitalOcean) with multi-AZ but no multi-region. No CDN. No serverless capabilities. Infrastructure is basic Droplets with Docker. Metadata lists Render but report only references DigitalOcean. Very small, simple infrastructure footprint suitable for an early-stage startup but lacks redundancy depth.

application_architecture.rationale: Monolithic architecture with FastAPI + PostgreSQL in Docker on Droplets. Only Python mentioned. Browser extension as primary delivery mechanism is novel but architecturally simple. Two environments (prod/UAT) is adequate. No mention of automated testing, typed languages, or microservices.

data_layer.rationale: Single database type (PostgreSQL) with S3-compatible object storage (DO Spaces). Daily backups with multi-AZ. Data classification and retention policies exist. No caching layer, no data warehouse, no mention of read replicas. Per-tenant segregation mentioned in controls (CC6.1.3). Basic but functional.

security_posture.rationale: Solid security fundamentals: MFA enforced, IDS/IPS via DigitalOcean, annual pen testing, monthly vulnerability scans, endpoint protection, firewalls, VPN for remote access, content filtering. Missing: no named WAF, no named SIEM, no specific TLS version stated. Cybersecurity insurance maintained. Good for company size.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC mentioned (baseline configurations as code, configuration management tool). Branch protection with merge approval required. Emergency change process documented. Separate environments. However, no named CI/CD tool, no container orchestration platform, no named source control provider. Docker used but no ECS/K8s.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing of loss scenarios with each scenario tested. Daily backups with multi-AZ. Failover by spinning up servers across AZs. However, no RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, and DR procedures are in Section 5 (not audited).

vendor_diversity.rationale: Extremely heavy concentration on a single vendor (DigitalOcean) for compute, storage, networking, IDS/IPS, key management, and password policy. No named monitoring, SIEM, or CI/CD vendors. Render mentioned in metadata but not in report. Critical single-vendor dependency creates significant risk.

overall.rationale: Early-stage startup with a simple but functional tech stack. Clean audit with zero exceptions (4 untestable due to no events during short 3-month period). Security fundamentals are in place. Main concerns: heavy DigitalOcean vendor concentration, short audit period (3 months), no named tooling for monitoring/SIEM/CI-CD, basic monolithic architecture, no multi-region capability. The 3-month audit window and lack of operational events (no incidents, no terminations, no significant changes) mean many controls were not truly battle-tested.

red_flags: Extremely short audit period (3 months: May 15 - Aug 15, 2025) - minimal operational history tested

4 controls could not be tested due to no events occurring during the engagement period (no incidents, no customer terminations, no significant personnel changes, no cyber incidents) - controls are untested in practice
Critical single-vendor dependency on DigitalOcean for all infrastructure, networking, IDS/IPS, key management, and password policies
No RTO/RPO disclosed despite BCDR policy existing
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded - concerning for a healthcare AI company handling clinical documentation
Metadata lists Render as infra_provider but report only mentions DigitalOcean - inconsistency suggests undisclosed infrastructure or metadata error
yellow_flags: No specific monitoring/APM, SIEM, or log aggregation tools named - raises question about actual tooling maturity
No named CI/CD pipeline tool - change management described procedurally but tooling unclear
No WAF mentioned despite being a web-facing application handling healthcare data
TLS version not specified - could be outdated
No caching layer for a production application
Very small team (CEO, COO, CTO with Growth Team and Product Team) - key person risk is high
DR procedures presented in Section 5 (not audited by independent auditor)
No mention of HIPAA-specific compliance controls despite being a healthcare company (beyond browser extension being HIPAA-compliant)
green_flags: Clean unqualified opinion with zero exceptions across all tested controls
MFA enforced across production access, remote access, source code, and email
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Branch protection with merge request approval required before production deployment
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and alerting
Data classification policy established and implemented
Access revoked within one business day of termination
Annual BCDR testing with documented scenarios
Vendor management program with SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments
Network segmentation with VPCs and ACLs
Formal change management with emergency change process
key_observations: The company metadata lists 'Render' as infrastructure provider but the SOC 2 report exclusively references DigitalOcean as the subservice organization - this discrepancy should be investigated
The network diagram reveals a simple two-tier architecture: Nginx reverse proxy -> FastAPI -> PostgreSQL (all Dockerized), which is straightforward but indicates early-stage architecture

Despite being an AI company automating clinical documentation from session recordings, there is zero mention of AI/ML infrastructure, model hosting, GPU compute, or audio processing pipelines in the SOC 2 report - these components may be out of scope or handled differently
The 3-month audit window is unusually short for a Type 2 report and resulted in 4 untestable controls - a 12-month period would provide substantially more assurance
The company has only 3 named executives (CEO, COO, CTO) suggesting a very early-stage startup where key person risk and separation of duties are inherent concerns
Password management is delegated to DigitalOcean's password policy rather than an enterprise identity provider, indicating limited IAM maturity

The browser extension delivery model (overlaying on existing EMR systems) means Perspective Health processes data within the client's environment, which has interesting security boundary implications not deeply explored in the report
